title: uragoga capitata
side_effects: Uragoga capitata can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting large quantities can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Prolonged use can also cause kidney damage and interact with medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. In severe cases, uragoga capitata poisoning can result in seizures, coma, and even death.
